mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show negative mean when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 9.0% indicates severe labor market slack and depressed consumer spending, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE > 9.0%, or t-stat for return difference is insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts signal broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.04) or interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side bottlenecks and rising input costs, compressing corporate margins and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or regime-conditional alpha is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings above 218.0 signal accelerating inflation that forces central banks to accelerate rate hikes, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL > 218.0, or threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the labor force and supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no predictive power for 60-day SPX direction (Rank IC < 0.03) or positive payroll changes are followed by negative returns in > 55% of months.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing quarterly GDP growth and persistently high unemployment indicates structural economic weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between GDPC1 growth and UNRATE fails to predict 20-day SPX direction (p > 0.10) or positive returns dominate when both signals align negatively.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and improving business confidence, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery phases are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or regime-specific predictive power is statistically insignificant.
